name: identify-content-gaps-and-inconsistencies
description: Identify gaps, errors, or inconsistencies in provided content

name: classify-claims-by-type
description: Extract and classify claims from source material by type: fact, opinion, or theory

name: evaluate-sources-by-reputation
description: Evaluate sources by reputation level, prioritising primary sources over secondary

name: identify-key-points-from-unstructured-material
description: Identify the most important points from unstructured source material

name: propose-explicit-thesis
description: Propose an explicit arguable thesis from source material, not just a topic description

name: perform-self-critique
description: Perform self-critique on completed work, identifying structural and logical weaknesses before delivering

name: make-reasoning-explicit
description: Connect conclusions to the evidence or decisions that produced them

name: identify-focal-point
description: Identify the single most important point before structuring output

name: calibrate-output-density
description: Calibrate output density and detail level to the audience's context and purpose

name: eliminate-off-focal-content
description: Eliminate content that does not advance the focal point of the output

name: evaluate-from-multiple-perspectives
description: Evaluate completed output from multiple distinct stakeholder or expert perspectives before delivering

name: adapt-vocabulary-to-audience
description: Adapt vocabulary and register to the audience's professional domain

name: extract-chronological-structure
description: Extract chronological structure from transcripts or meeting notes

name: distinguish-substance-from-structure
description: Distinguish substantive results (decisions, conclusions) from structural results (frameworks, process insights that emerged)

name: synthesise-from-multiple-source-types
description: Synthesise information from multiple source types, weighting by interpretive priority

name: provide-context-on-first-mention
description: Provide brief context for every named person, concept, or technical term on its first appearance in the output

name: maintain-consistent-terminology
description: Use the same term for the same concept throughout the output; audit for semantic drift

name: claim-verification-report
description: Return a verification report classifying each claim as: Verified (2+ high-reputation sources), Questionable (1 high-reputation or multiple lower-quality sources), or Not Verifiable (no credible sources found), with source links for each

name: verification-summary
description: Return a summary with: total claim count, count per verification status, percentage per status, and brief analysis of patterns

name: structured-essay-outline
description: Return a numbered outline with: one explicit arguable thesis, section summaries (one sentence each) explaining connection to thesis, and an explicit list of excluded material with reasons

name: content-sourcing-summary
description: Return a sourcing summary tracking the proportion of output derived from: direct quotation, paraphrase, elaboration, and generated content

name: document-content-ratio
description: Return written output targeting this content ratio: description (25%), analysis (50%), recommendations (25%)

name: prefer-source-quality-over-quantity
description: When source quality and source quantity conflict: prefer one authoritative primary source over multiple lower-reputation sources

name: report-source-conflicts-without-resolving
description: When authoritative sources conflict on the same claim: present both perspectives with sources; mark as uncertain; do not select a winner

name: classify-uncertain-claims-conservatively
description: When uncertain whether a statement qualifies as a claim: classify it as a claim rather than omitting it

name: prefer-omission-over-fabrication
description: When content integrity and writing quality conflict: prefer omission or explicit acknowledgement over fabricating details not present in the source material

name: structure-before-prose
description: When structure and prose quality conflict: complete and validate the structure before drafting prose

name: state-weaknesses-directly
description: When evaluating completed work: state weaknesses directly without qualifying praise; describe problems, not improvements

name: cut-generic-before-vivid
description: When compressing output: cut generic or transitional content before cutting vivid specifics, the key surprise or turn, or any moment of emotional engagement

name: explain-before-label
description: When introducing a concept: describe the mechanism and explain why it matters before naming it

name: use-concrete-before-abstract
description: When introducing an unfamiliar concept: lead with specific concrete examples before stating the definition or framework

name: plant-before-reveal
description: When including a surprising conclusion or turn: introduce a signal of it early so the reveal feels like a payoff, not a pivot

name: reproduce-failure-before-investigating
description: Reproduce the failure consistently before investigating its cause

name: compare-working-and-broken-examples
description: Compare working and broken examples to identify the specific differences causing failure

name: form-single-root-cause-hypothesis
description: Form a single explicit hypothesis about root cause before attempting a fix

name: test-one-variable-at-a-time
description: Make the smallest possible change to test each hypothesis; change one variable at a time

name: read-error-messages-completely
description: Read error messages and stack traces completely, noting line numbers, file paths, and error codes

name: trace-errors-to-origin
description: Trace errors backward from where they manifest to their point of origin

name: add-observation-points-at-boundaries
description: Add observation points at each component boundary to identify where in a system failure occurs

name: write-failing-test-first
description: Write a failing test before writing any implementation code

name: verify-test-fails-correctly
description: Verify that each test fails for the expected reason before writing implementation

name: write-minimal-passing-code
description: Write the minimal code needed to make a failing test pass; do not add features beyond what the test requires

name: run-verification-before-claiming-complete
description: Run the verification command and read the full output before claiming a task is complete

name: propose-multiple-approaches
description: Propose 2-3 distinct approaches with trade-offs and a clear recommendation before settling on one

name: ask-one-question-at-a-time
description: Ask one clarifying question at a time; wait for the answer before asking the next

name: decompose-into-small-tasks
description: Decompose work into tasks small enough to complete and verify independently

name: verify-changes-against-current-state
description: Verify proposed changes against the current state of the system or codebase before implementing

name: implement-one-change-at-a-time
description: Implement changes one at a time and verify each before proceeding to the next

name: identify-independent-tasks
description: Identify which tasks can proceed without shared state or sequential dependencies before deciding how to sequence work

name: scope-subagent-tasks-narrowly
description: Assign each subagent a single clear problem domain, all context needed, and explicit constraints on scope

name: verify-outputs-before-integrating
description: Verify that outputs from parallel work streams do not conflict before integrating them

name: implementation-plan-with-test-steps
description: Return an implementation plan with: goal (one sentence), architecture description (2-3 sentences), technology stack, and numbered tasks each specifying exact file paths, complete code, verification commands with expected output, and a commit step

name: confirm-root-cause-before-fixing
description: When a fix or solution is proposed: confirm root cause first; do not address symptoms without understanding their source

name: stop-after-repeated-failures
description: When 3 or more fix attempts have failed: stop and evaluate whether the approach itself is sound before attempting another

name: fix-at-source-not-symptom
description: When a failure has a known root cause: fix at the source; do not address symptoms while the cause remains

name: test-first-always
description: When implementing a feature or fix: write the failing test first; delete any implementation written before the test was in place

name: simplify-when-hard-to-test
description: When a test is hard to write: simplify the interface being tested; difficulty testing indicates difficulty using

name: evidence-before-completion-claim
description: When about to claim a task is complete or a problem is resolved: verify with direct evidence first; state what the evidence shows, not what you expect it to show

name: state-limitations-explicitly
description: When verification evidence is unavailable: state the limitation explicitly; do not substitute confidence for evidence

name: clarify-before-implementing
description: When asked to implement: clarify purpose, constraints, and success criteria first; do not implement until these are confirmed

name: omit-unverified-features
description: When designing or reviewing: if a feature has no verified use case in the current scope, omit it

name: dispatch-parallel-when-independent
description: When multiple tasks are independent and have no shared state: work on them in parallel rather than sequentially
